events.title: Iran Strikes Prince Sultan Air Base
events.desc: Iranian drones and missiles struck Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia, destroying an E-3 Sentry and damaging multiple KC-135 tankers. This is part of a broader counterair campaign waged through asymmetric means in the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran.
events.location: Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia

events.title: US Deploys Mines over Iranian Village
events.desc: The US appears to have deployed the Gator Scatterable Mine system over Kafari, a village near Shiraz, in southern Iran. Several people were killed according to Iranian media.

events.title: Iranian Strikes on IRGC Compound and School in Minab
events.desc: Two new videos show strikes on an Iranian Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) compound and an adjacent school in Minab. The videos confirm multiple missiles hitting the complex.

events.title: U.S. Blockades Iranian Ports in Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: The U.S. announced a blockade on vessels calling at Iranian ports in the Strait of Hormuz, aiming to deny Iran vital trade and further pressure its oil supply to Asia. This move raises fears of a fresh oil shock.

events.title: Iranian Government Imposes Internet Blackout
events.desc: The Iranian government deliberately dismantled its communications infrastructure and halted internet traffic, causing a near-total blackout. This action was imposed on the Iranian people following strikes by adversaries.

events.title: Israel-Lebanon Direct Talks in the US
events.desc: Envoys from Lebanon and Israel are meeting in the US for direct talks, the first since 1993. However, Hezbollah's rejection of a ceasefire threatens the US-brokered effort.

events.title: France, UK to Co-host Talks on Hormuz Mission
events.desc: France and Britain will co-host a video conference for countries willing to contribute to a defensive multilateral mission to restore freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz. The meeting aims to address security conditions and maritime transit.

events.title: US-Israeli War Impacts Global Food Security
events.desc: The war in Iran has exposed urea, a key fertilizer, as a strategic commodity. Disruptions to its supply chain and a potential closure of the Strait of Hormuz risk a global food security crisis, impacting staple grain production.

events.title: Hungary Government Passwords Exposed Online
events.desc: Approximately 800 Hungarian government email addresses and passwords have been exposed online, revealing vulnerabilities in ministries handling sensitive information. 12 out of 13 ministries were affected.

events.title: Russia's Lavrov Visits China Amidst Middle East Crisis
events.desc: Russian Foreign Minister Sergei Lavrov arrived in China for talks concerning the wars in Ukraine and Iran, as well as bilateral relations. China and Russia have been critical of the US-Israel war on Iran.

events.title: Italy Suspends Defence Cooperation Deal with Israel
events.desc: Italy's government has suspended the automatic renewal of a defence cooperation deal with Israel, citing the conflicts in the Middle East. This decision comes despite Italy being a close ally of Israel.

events.title: Iran Carries Out Highest Number of Executions in Decades
events.desc: Human rights organizations warn that Iran may execute many more people following the anti-government protests earlier this year. The current number of executions is the highest in decades.

events.title: Israeli Strikes Intensify on Lebanon Amidst Hezbollah Attacks
events.desc: Israel has escalated strikes on Lebanon, with over 160 people killed since Friday. Hezbollah attacks continue despite the US-Iran ceasefire, indicating a persistent conflict on this front.

events.title: Palestinian Shot Dead During Israeli Settler Attack in West Bank
events.desc: A Palestinian was killed during an attack by Israeli settlers on a village in the occupied West Bank. This incident occurred amid warnings from ex-security chiefs about escalating 'government sponsored Jewish terrorism'.

events.title: Lebanon and Israel Officials Meet in the US Amidst Ongoing Conflict
events.desc: Lebanese and Israeli envoys are set to meet in Washington for talks. However, a Lebanese official stated that Lebanon would only participate if a ceasefire were in place, highlighting the unresolved tensions.
